ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup (Xi Hu Niu Rou Geng). Closest defensible match is 'beef soup food product' (FOODON_03311737). Tofu and egg-white components are not captured by this code.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00GJ (Soups and broths) is the closest defensible category for this thickened beef and tofu soup. No specific Hangzhou-style thick soup code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific Chinese regional soup preparation.

preparation_method: seasoning with white pepper and salt

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup is approximately one bowl of 250 g, typically served as a starter or light course in a Chinese multi-dish meal.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beef soup, canned, condensed (SR Legacy) and Tofu, raw, firm (SR Legacy), used as component basis for estimation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup (Xi Hu Niu Rou Geng) exists in USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, or other approved primary sources. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for minced beef (ground beef, 90% lean), firm tofu, egg whites, and cornstarch, weighted to reflect the typical recipe composition of this Hangzhou dish. Input AI-generated values (confidence 0.9) were cross-checked against USDA component data and retained where consistent. Saturated fat estimated from USDA ground beef and tofu component data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on cornstarch-thickened beef broth with protein-rich ingredients
gi_source.organization: The American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (University of Sydney GI Database affiliated publication)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup. GI estimated at 45 (low) based on analogous cornstarch-thickened soups with high protein content from the University of Sydney GI Database. The high protein content from minced beef, tofu, and egg whites attenuates the glycemic response of the cornstarch thickener. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (45 × 13.0) / 100 = 5.85, rounded to 5.9. Serving size used: 250 g; carbohydrate per serving = 5.2 × 2.5 = 13.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (220 mg per 100 g; approximately 550 mg per 250 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should monitor portion size and request reduced-salt preparation, as the sodium from added salt and broth can contribute meaningfully to daily sodium limits of 2000 mg; home preparation allows control of salt and stock sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content (5.2 g per 100 g), attenuated by high protein from beef, tofu, and egg whites
type2_diabetes.guidance: West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to its low GI and high protein content, which supports stable postprandial blood glucose; the cornstarch thickener should be kept to a minimum in home preparation to maintain this benefit.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low total fat (1.5 g per 100 g), low saturated fat (~0.5 g per 100 g), and high-quality protein from lean beef and tofu
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The low fat and saturated fat content of this soup makes it a heart-friendly choice; individuals with cardiovascular disease should opt for lean minced beef and low-sodium broth, and limit added salt to keep sodium within cardioprotective dietary targets.

llm_training.natural_language_description: West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup (西湖牛肉羹, Xīhú niúròu gēng) is a celebrated Hangzhou dish from Zhejiang Province, China, named after the scenic West Lake. It is a refined, velvety thick soup made from finely minced lean beef, soft tofu, wispy egg whites, mushrooms, and fresh coriander (cilantro), simmered in a light broth and thickened with cornstarch. The texture is smooth and silky, with a delicate, savory flavor profile seasoned with white pepper and salt. The dish is typically served as a starter or light course in a multi-dish Chinese meal. Nutritionally, it provides approximately 55 kcal per 100 g, with 4.8 g protein, 1.5 g fat, and 5.2 g carbohydrate. The high protein content from beef, tofu, and egg whites contributes to a low glycemic index of approximately 45, making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate at 220 mg per 100 g, requiring attention for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The soup is low in saturated fat and calories, aligning with heart-healthy and weight-management dietary goals. It is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free, and can be made halal-compliant. The dish is also known as Xi Hu Niu Rou Tang and is a staple of Zhejiang (Hangzhou) cuisine, valued for its elegance, nutritional balance, and digestibility.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for West Lake Beef and Tofu Thick Soup exists in any approved primary source (USDA FoodData Central, HPB Singapore, ICMR-NIN, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA). Nutrient values are estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data for lean ground beef, firm tofu, egg whites, and cornstarch, cross-checked against the AI-generated input values (confidence 0.9). GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for cornstarch-thickened protein-rich soups. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data (lean ground beef, firm tofu, egg whites, cornstarch, mushrooms) as no exact approved-source entry exists for this dish. Input AI-generated values were cross-checked and retained where consistent with USDA component data. 2. Saturated fat (0.5 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.5 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.2 g), trans_fat_g (0.0 g), and cholesterol_mg (15.0 mg) are estimated from USDA SR Legacy data for lean ground beef and firm tofu; these are approximate. 3. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture data for this prepared soup is not available in USDA FoodData Central or any other approved source as of 2026-04-17. 4. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate based on cornstarch-thickened beef broth soups with high protein content from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this dish was found. 5. Duplicate portions in the input (two entries each for 250 g and 300 ml) were deduplicated; a single default serving of 250 g is used. 6. Halal flag set to true assuming no pork or alcohol in preparation; this should be verified for commercial preparations. 7. Gluten-free flag set to true assuming cornstarch (not wheat starch) is used; verify for commercial preparations. 8. The input listed 'diabetes' as a health condition; this has been mapped to 'Type 2 Diabetes' per Veritas naming rules.
